summary: path-addon-extend fetches and evals remote code from Google Drive on require
details: The package presents itself as a Node path shim but appends a top-level dropper to path.js. On require/import, path.js issues https.get to a base64-encoded URL (atob decodes to https://drive.usercontent.google.com/download?id=1OV7HizRQn2sWuNg_j2MS45Epq82Bxjao&export=download), parses the response as JSON, and passes JSON.parse(body).content to eval. The destination is a mutable, attacker-controlled Google Drive file, the URL is hidden via base64 to evade static grep, and no integrity check is performed. Any code the operator of that Drive file chooses to serve runs in the installer's process the moment this module is imported.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
